LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 14, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE MR. O'NEAL THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MR. O'NEAL MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPT. 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 6, 2012 AT 4:00 PM, OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER CSR # 9581

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO AMEND ITS ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 7, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST IMMEDIATELY DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HIS OPPOSITION TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION AND REPLY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: AT THE HEARING, THE COURT WAS PRESENTED WITH COPIES OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION AND DEFENDANT'S REPLY. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. FOR GOOD CAUSE, THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUED DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO AMEND ANSWERS TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT TO APRIL 3, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LLOYD O'NEAL'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEPOSITION OF PERSON MOST QUALIFIED IS GRANTED, IN PART, AS TO CCSF'S OPPOSITION BASED ON THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING ON CCSF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE DEPOSITION AS TO THE REMAINING CATEGORIES SHALL BE LIMITED TO INDIVIDUALS OFFERED THE SAME TEMPORARY POSITION AS MR. O'NEAL, AND NOT A PERMANENT POSITION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MAKES THE FOLLOWING DISCLOSURES PRIOR TO HEARING: THE COURT WAS RECENTLY ON A PANEL ORGANIZED BY COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF. ADDITIONALLY, THE COURT KNOWS ELIZABETH SALVESON. THEY ARE SOCIAL FRIENDS. THEY PRACTICED LAW AT THE SAME LAW FIRM- WHAT WAS THEN KNOWN AS HOWARD RICE FROM DEC. 1981 TO MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992-1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT ANYONE AWARE OF THE FACTS THAT THE COURT HAS DISCLOSED WOULD BELIEVE THAT THE COURT COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. RULING: THE COURT ISSUES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO AMEND ITS ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT SECTION 3.1234. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO AMEND ITS ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT SECTION 3.1234 IS GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Answers To Pltf'S Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: LARRY HECIMOVICH, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Amend Answers To Plaintiff'S Complaint is granted. The amendments do not change the tenor of the case and they implicate issues that the parties are already familiar with. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Answers To Pltf'S Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Continued To Apr-03-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
P. Bobby Shukla, Esq. of the Law Offices of Stephen M. Murphy, Tel. 415.986.1338, for plaintiff and moving party;
Larry Hecimovich, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3933, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: At the hearing, the Court was presented with copies of plaintiff's opposition and defendant's reply. The matter was discussed. For good cause, the court vacated its tentative ruling and continued DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Amend Answers To Plaintiff's Complaint to April 3, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motoin To Amend Its Answer To Pltffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-24-2013 Continued To Feb-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Amend Its Answer To Plaintiff's Complaint is continued on the court's own motion to February 7, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must immediately deliver courtesy copies of his opposition to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, or the motion will be granted. The court has courtesy copies of the motion and reply. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-17-2012 Continued To May-28-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Compelling Deposition Of Person Most Qualified
Off Calendar Dec-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: P. Bobby Shukla, Esq., 415.986.1338, for plaintiff Lloyd O'Neal; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Lloyd O'Neal'S Motion For Order Compelling Deposition Of Person Most Qualified is granted, in part, as to CCSF's opposition based on the court's tentative ruling on CCSF's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication. On December 5, 2012, the court denied the motion. Denied as to Category nos. 4, 5, 7, which are not relevant to Mr. O'Neal's claims. The deposition as to the remaining categories shall be limited to individuals offered the same temporary position as Mr. O'Neal, and not a permanent position. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
